response: MIT, founded in 1861, is ranked as the #1 university in the world and boasts an extremely selective acceptance rate of just 3.9%. The institution serves 11,500 students annually with a tuition of $57,590, supported by an impressive $24.6 billion endowment and a faculty of 1,069 members. Notably, MIT has produced 98 Nobel laureates, cementing its reputation as a leading center for academic excellence and research innovation.
